app.title: Customer Service Knowledge Assistant
app.description: Manage support documents, SOP rules, and AI response playbooks in one workspace for search, Q&A, and service operations.

home.hero_title: Put knowledge, SOPs, and reply strategies into one support workspace
home.hero_description: Reuse the existing document parsing and retrieval stack for support FAQs, product guidance, standard replies, and AI handling playbooks.
home.search_placeholder: Search docs, FAQs, or file names...
home.empty_hint: Start by uploading FAQs, product guides, refund policies, or support training materials.

panel_items.sla: Answer consistently around SLA, escalation, and compensation rules
panel_items.handoff: Move complex issues from bot to human quickly
panel_items.consistency: Keep every support channel aligned on the same knowledge

workflow_items.faq: FAQ, refund, replacement, and after-sales rules
workflow_items.product: Product manuals, usage guides, pricing, and campaign notes
workflow_items.sop: Escalation flows, ticket rules, and QA standards

form.title: Document Title
form.description_placeholder: Describe where support agents should use this document...
form.document: Document (PDF, Word, Markdown, CSV, Excel, HTML)
form.drop_text: Click or drag a file to upload

chat.title: Support AI Assistant
chat.subtitle: Knowledge-grounded answers and handling guidance for support teams
chat.empty_state: Ask about refund rules, escalation flows, product functions, or standard replies.
chat.placeholder: Ask a support question, for example: How should we reply to a refund request?
chat.error: The request failed. Please try again later.
chat.clear_confirm: Clear all chat history? This cannot be undone.

quick.refund: A user requests a refund. How should support reply and explain the timeline?
quick.escalation: When should the issue be escalated to tier 2 or a supervisor?
quick.complaint: How should support calm a customer complaining about service attitude?
quick.activation: A new customer cannot activate the product. How should support guide them?

capabilities.title: SOP and Reply Library
capabilities.description: Manage support SOPs, escalation rules, standard replies, and QA checkpoints for both AI and human agents.
capabilities.empty_hint: Add your first SOP or standard reply.
capabilities.search_placeholder: Search SOP names, descriptions, or key steps...

form.name: SOP Name
form.description_placeholder: Describe the issue type, prerequisites, and expected outcome...
form.features_placeholder: One step or rule per line
form.benefits_placeholder: One target or QA checkpoint per line
form.specs_placeholder: Optional JSON for field mappings or ticket params
form.performance_placeholder: Optional JSON for SLA, timing, or escalation thresholds

capabilities.duplicate_error: This name already exists. Please use a different one.

drafts.title: Response Playbooks
drafts.subtitle: Editable support reply drafts and handling plans generated from your knowledge base and SOPs.
drafts.empty_hint: Create a playbook for refunds, complaints, activation issues, escalation, and more.

generator.title: AI Response Playbook Generator
generator.description: Describe the support case and the system will generate standard replies, handling steps, and escalation guidance.
generator.requirements_placeholder:
Describe the customer issue, current sentiment, actions already taken, and the desired support outcome.

Example: The customer received a damaged item and wants a refund while also complaining about logistics. Support needs a calming reply, refund explanation, and escalation guidance.
generator.industry_placeholder: e.g. Ecommerce after-sales, SaaS subscription, voice support
generator.customer_type_placeholder: e.g. New user, VIP, enterprise account
generator.expected_features_placeholder: e.g. calming reply, verification checklist, timing explanation, transfer conditions, compensation guidance
generator.additional_notes_placeholder: Add constraints such as regulation, risk level, budget, or special handling rules.
generator.requirements_error: Please fill in the case background first

tips.specific: The more specific the case background is, the better the result fits real support work.
tips.context: Add customer tier, order status, and SLA constraints to tighten the reply strategy.
tips.editable: Generated playbooks should still be reviewed by a human before frontline use.
tips.retry: If the result is weak, add clearer constraints and generate again.
